calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar May-04-2006 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: JPT: David May, Clerk: Karissa Shaw, Not reported. (525)

APPEARANCES:
Christiane Grando, Appellant, appearing in Pro Per.
Nancy Hestand, Respondent, appearing in Pro Per.

This cause comes on regularly for trial de novo. All litigants are sworn.

The Court hears three related cases together, #028093, #028094, and #028095.

The Court questions the litigants as to the factual issues of the case.

The Court allows the litigants to give statements regarding what was said during each other's testimony.

The following Appellant's Exhibits are ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE;
Ex. 1 - 2 page document titled 'Original Hearing Issues'
Ex. 2 - Letter from Appellant to the San Francisco Superior Court
Ex. 3 - 1 page document titled 'Responsibilities'
Ex. 4 - 1 page document titled 'Case NO T020288'
Ex. 5 - 8 page Decision from the Residential Rent Stabilization Board
Ex. 6 - 3 page document titled 'Rent and Deposit Issues'
Ex. 7 - 1 page untitled document advertising a room for rent
Ex. 8 - 1 page document titled 'Responsibilities'
Ex. 9 - 10 page e-mail communication between roommates

The following exhibits presented by litigant Hestand are ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE;
Ex. 1 - E-mail from Grando to Hestand titled ' Noticed Received', dated 11/9/04
Ex. 2 - E-mail from Hestand to Grando titled 'Confirmation Received', dated 11/9/04
Ex. 3 - E-mail from Grando to Hestand titled 'Walk Through', dated 12/2/04
Ex. 4 - Letter written by Hestand to Grando, dated 11/6/04

The court accepts 2 folders filled with documents from litigant James despite Ms. James statement that these documents were unnecessary due to the evidence already presented by litigant Hestand. The only documents contained in these folders are typed notes by Ms. James, copies of Rental Board flyers and copies of documents from the original small claims file #815501.

The Court hears closing arguments and the matter is taken under submission.

JUDGMENT RENDERED.
